channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bluesky photo-sharing app Flashes launches in beta
item.description: Flashes, a photo-sharing app that’s linked to X-alternative Bluesky, launched in beta this week before disappearing from the test store due to what its developer said were technical difficulties.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups
item.description: GSA senior leaders are saying the Trump administration’s long-term goal is to merge GSA, OPM and OMB into a single agency. The post GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple built a super-cute, expressive robot lamp that is giving us major Pixar vibes
item.description: A new research paper from Apple Machine Learning just revealed it has built a robot lamp prototype that is super-cute and reminiscent of Pixar's Luxo Jr. lamp.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon reports strong earnings for Q4, but stocks dip due to guidance for the 1st quarter
item.description: Amazon on Thursday reported better-than-expected revenue and profits for the holiday shopping period.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Contract intelligence comes to PDF
item.description: According to a new Adobe Acrobat survey, 89% of knowledge workers encounter contracts on the job, with more than half (52%) saying they work with contracts at least weekly.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Hundreds of rigged votes can skew AI model rankings on Chatbot Arena, study finds
item.description: The generative AI revolution has turned into a global race, with mixtures of models from private companies and open-source initiatives all competing to become the most popular and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft reveals more on just how much it'll cost you to keep using Windows 10
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed who will be eligible for the Windows 10 ESU program, and exactly how much it’ll cost.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La falta de formación no merma la concepción estratégica de la IA
item.description: Con el punto y final de un 2024 marcado por la irrupción popular de la inteligencia artificial (IA), se podría decir que este nuevo año supondrá la consagración de la tecnología en el plano político, empresarial y social.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NetSuite adds new AI capabilities to improve enterprise workflows
item.description: NetSuite this week continued to add new AI capabilities to its suite offering that are expected to help enterprises expand their customization capabilities and improve AI-assisted workflows.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIOs: Catalysts to promote sustainability through collaboration
item.description: Sustainability initiatives are largely driven by data, and CIOs are uniquely positioned to ensure their organizations have the right data necessary to drive collaborative decision-making practices that influence sustainability.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US Companies Outpace Europe in IT Investments
item.description: Large US companies invest 10% more in technology and IT compared to their European counterparts, highlighting a significant gap in competitiveness.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have been abandoning or scaling back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: A grainy surveillance photo of an assault suspect on St. Louis' Metrolink led a computer facial recognition program to identify Chris Gatlin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: With ‘Air,’ Opera launches a browser to reduce stress
item.description: If you get stressed out when surfing the web, Norwegian browser company Opera has something for you: a new browser for Windows and Mac designed to help you relax. Opera Air offers a minimalist design and scheduled breaks for breathing exercises, neck training and meditation, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI takes big step forward with 3D-printed shoe
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI and scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by the Vesuvius volcano
item.description: U.K. scientists say they have made a historic breakthrough by making the first image of the inside of a scroll carbonized by a volcanic eruption 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Pan Pylas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DeepSeek's rise raises data privacy, national security concerns
item.description: The emergence of a newly popular artificial intelligence (AI) model from Chinese startup DeepSeek is raising national security and data privacy concerns for the U.S., not unlike those that spurred a ban on TikTok last month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EU supports AI challenge to Silicon Valley and China
item.description: The European Union is backing a major AI initiative, OpenEuroLLM, in a bid to counter the dominance of Silicon Valley and China in artificial intelligence. The initiative is being led by Jan Hajič, a computational linguist at Charles University in Czechia, and Peter Sarlin, co-founder of Silo AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
